title: satureja troodi subsp. vardarana
intro: Satureja troodi subsp. vardarana, also known as Macedonian savory, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its essential oils. The health benefits of Satureja troodi subsp. vardarana include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, improve digestion, and even exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant is often prepared as a tea, essential oil, or dried herb for use in traditional medicine and culinary purposes.
